replies.author: @returnspirit
replies.text: ​It is worth delving deeper into this research, for the first writing system was revealed by Thoth; to understand the system of governance, one must first grasp the divine natural order with natural laws that maintain balance with nature, and this knowledge known as *Maat*. It was Thoth who first articulated the knowledge of *Maat* and illuminated spirituality; he recorded this wisdom on stone tablets, transmitting the wisdom of Kemet to Babylon, and then from Babylon to Sumer. The names "Egypt," "Iraq," or "Iran" Israel did not exist in antiquity, and name Rome was merely a village during the time of Nimrod who ruled over Babylon (located today between Iraq and Syria) Roman empire, get they name from that village, and nimrod also are  descendant of Kush. History does not vanish; what nature has storege is beyond human knowledge, and what people fail to grasp is that they have never realized that divine creation itself possesses a memory that bears witness to and records everything. These new names were introduced by a Greek leader named Solon who had traveled to Kemet to study divine laws for fifteen years. He introduced these names not out of malice, but to make them easier for his own people to understand. However, after Greece, it was the Roman Empire's turn to invade Greece, Kush, Kemet, and Judah (modern-day Israel), occupying innocent lands and forcibly destroying these natural principles. Let us recall that it was Roman generals who ordered the torture and crucifixion of Issa (Jesus); yet Jesus himself had been schooled in the tradition of Kemet.  Although the Romans claim to have acted with the approval of Jewish authorities and assert that Jesus died for the sins of humanity, they never acknowledge their own sins; instead, they justify their actions by citing an alleged divine will. Yet, they claim to follow Jesus while perpetuating the mindset of the Roman Empire: they build churches and mosques, spread corruption on earth, shatter human unity and civilization, subvert the collective conscience, and distort natural laws and human spirituality through invasion, colonialism, occupation, terrorism, jihadism, torture, rape, and lies in order to enslave others.They continue to employ the same tactics such as crucifixion against those who reject them; it is this very mindset that remains responsible for the devastation wrought upon the earth. pay attention to cross it was Roman torture tool, because anger of  the ankh after leaving kamet without ability to destroy the ankh Roman claim to see vision from sky cross the musine Mountain, now known as Italy,for more understand, history pay attention to culture, village rules system, lifestyle, language, traditional dress tools, spirituality

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Good distinction to draw — "civilization" here refers to organized cities, writing, and government, not the origin of humanity itself, which goes back much further and mostly to East Africa. Mesopotamia is credited as one of the earliest places those specific building blocks came together.

comments.text: As an Iraqi, and as a descendant of a civilization that gave rise to great leaders and kings throughout history, I extend my deepest gratitude and heartfelt appreciation to everyone who contributed to the creation of this remarkable documentary about my country and my people. replies.author: @simocast
replies.text: You took all the right words out of my mouth. As an Iraqi born man, and one who knows little about our history and heritage, this is truly amazing to see and learn. Thank you for the recognition and education.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Your words mean a great deal to us. Iraq carries the weight of civilization itself! Mesopotamia gave humanity writing, law, mathematics, the very idea of cities — and yet so much of the modern world has forgotten where it came from. Telling this story with the dignity your country deserves was the whole point. Thank you for watching, and for honoring it with such a generous comment. 🙏

replies.text: You're raising a fair point... 7 thousand years of overlapping civilizations can't be fully covered in a single documentary. We try to be as careful with the history as the format allows, but there's always more to the story than any one film can hold.

comments.author: @msa_2175
comments.text: The world may rewrite history a thousand times, but it can never erase where civilization truly began. Iraq 🇮🇶 — the land of Mesopotamia — gave humanity its first writing, first laws, first cities, and the foundations of knowledge itself. While others were still searching for survival, Iraq was teaching the world how to build civilization. They tried to destroy its libraries, bury its history, and diminish its role, yet the truth remains unshaken: the roots of human civilization were planted in Iraq, and no one on earth can erase the legacy of the land that helped awaken humanity.🇮🇶♥️

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Well said. That's really the heart of it... the land that taught the world to write couldn't be written out of history, no matter how many times someone tried.

replies.author: @AG2000W
replies.text:
Arabs have been present in Mesopotamia since the time of the Assyrians across several regions, such as the Kingdom of Hatra in Nineveh, alongside Arab tribes in the deserts of Anbar, Salah al-Din, and southern Iraq. It is worth noting that the majority of the Arab population in modern Iraq consists of Arabized indigenous peoples whose culture transformed following centuries of Islamic rule over these areas. Even during the pre-Islamic Persian rule of Iraq, most inhabitants of central and southern Iraq belonged to Arab or Arabized tribes.
As for Baghdad, Diyala, Kirkuk, Nineveh, and Dohuk, their inhabitants were predominantly Assyrian and Chaldean. Iraq has long been a Semitic land for Assyrians, Chaldeans, Arabs, and Jews.
In contrast, Persians and Iranian peoples, such as the Medes and Kurds, originate from Iran and have no ties to the civilizations of Mesopotamia. They are indigenous to Iran, not Anatolia, the Levant, or Mesopotamia. Even the areas they currently inhabit, such as Erbil, served as religious capitals for the Assyrians before the Kurds committed genocide against the Assyrians, Armenians, and Yazidis in those regions and colonized them :

1-The Massacres of Badr Khan (1843–1846)
• Victims: 10,000–30,000 Assyrians
• Location: Hakkari and Tur Abdin 
2-The Hamidian Massacres (1894–1896)
• Victims: 100,000–300,000 Armenians and Assyrians
• Location: Eastern Anatolia
3-The Armenian Genocide (1915–1923)
• Victims: 1,000,000–1,500,000 Armenians
• Location: Eastern Anatolia and the Levant 
4-The Assyrian Genocide / Seyfo (1915–1923)
• Victims: 150,000–300,000 Assyrians
• Location: Southeastern Anatolia and Northwestern Persia
5-The Simele Massacre (1933)
• Victims: 3,000–6,000 Assyrians
• Location: Northern Iraq (Kingdom of Iraq)
6-The Soran Emirate Genocide / Firmana Mîrê Kor (1832–1834)  
• Victims: 70,000–135,000 Yazidis  
• Location: Sheikhan, Sinjar, and Erbil (Ottoman Empire)  
7-The Bedir Khan Massacres (1832–1844)  
• Victims: 12,000–70,000 Yazidis
• Location: Sheikhan, Khatarah, and Tur Abdin (Ottoman Empire)  
8-The Hamidiye Cavalry Massacres (1894–1923)  
• Victims: Up to 300,000 Yazidis   
• Location: Eastern Anatolia and Transcaucasia (Ottoman Empire)
